bizcity_crm_flows.purpose: Intent flow definitions (port from legacy wp_bizgpt_custom_flows). Each row = 1 trigger keyword + action (run_shortcode | send_message) + optional reminder. Consumed by BizCity_CG_Flow_Handler when inbound message arrives.

message_khong_dau.comment: Trigger keyword normalized (no tones) for fuzzy match

prompt.comment: LLM prompt OR raw message text depending on reply_mode

reply_mode.comment: send_message branch only: direct (raw text) | llm (generate via LLM Client)

output_json.comment: Reserved: cached last LLM JSON parse result

delay_only.comment: If 1, do not run action, only schedule reminder

updated_at.type: DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

history.change: Initial port from legacy wp_bizgpt_custom_flows. Adds reply_mode column (direct|llm) for send_message action branch (task #2).

Renamed table bizcity_cg_flows → bizcity_crm_flows (align with bizcity_crm_* CRM prefix family). Canonical from this point on: ONLY bizcity_crm_flows is acceptable in code/docs. Migration installer performs atomic RENAME (DROP empty dst + RENAME TABLE) to avoid duplicate tables. Options bizcity_cg_flows_* are mirrored once into bizcity_crm_flows_*.

Runtime hardening (NO schema change). (a) bizcity-facebook-bot webhook handler `handle_referral()` now SKIPS legacy `bizgpt_run_flow_steps($id)` when the decrypted ref resolves to a Campaign (Campaign_Tracker + Scenario_Dispatcher already own the response via `bizcity_facebook_referral_received` action). Fixes: `Table 'slave1.wp_1258_bizcity_crm_flows' doesn't exist` on bizcity.vn blog 1258 where scenarios live exclusively in `bizcity_crm_campaigns`. (b) Legacy `bizgpt_run_flow_steps()` wraps the SELECT in `suppress_errors(true)` so missing-table on satellite sites no longer floods debug.log. (c) `BizCity_CRM_Campaign_Ref_Codec::decode()` adds path #0 for plain-numeric input (so `ref_decrypted` from webhook adapter resolves directly through `campaign_id` then `imported_from_bizgpt_flow_id`). (d) QR endpoints `/bizcity-crm/v1/campaigns/{id}/qr.{png,svg}` switched to `__return_true` permission so `` (no X-WP-Nonce header) renders correctly. (e) Legacy plugin `bizgpt-custom-flows` repointed at canonical `bizcity_crm_flows` (no longer creates `bizgpt_custom_flows` on activation) — see `wp-content/plugins/bizgpt-custom-flows/PHASE-LEGACY-DEPRECATED.md`.

Runtime hardening (NO schema change). `BizCity_CRM_Campaign_Tracker::record_visit()` is now archived-tolerant for scenario dispatch. Previously, hitting an archived campaign returned `WP_Error('bizcity_crm_track_archived')` → no visit inserted → `Scenario_Dispatcher::on_visit_recorded` never cached envelope → first inbound message produced ONLY generic CRM AI reply (notebook fallback), NOT the campaign scenario template. Symptom in logs: `record_visit_result.wp_error='campaign is archived'` followed by `scenario_dispatcher.envelope_miss` for the contact's first message. New behavior: emit `crm_campaign_visit_recorded` event with `archived_dispatch=true` + `visit_id=0` (mirror of dedupe-revisit branch), skip the `bizcity_crm_campaign_visits` INSERT (funnel stays clean), and return 0 instead of WP_Error so caller `Campaign_Tracker::on_fb_referral_received()` logs at info level. `Conversion_Linker.link_visit()` is idempotent (existing converted_contact_id short-circuits), so re-emitting per referral is safe. Operational note: if user wants to truly stop the scenario, they must delete/replace the FB ad creative OR delete the campaign row (status='archived' is no longer enough on its own).

migrations.note: Auto on admin Flows page load. dst is just-created by ensure_table() and is empty, so DROP is safe. RENAME is atomic from app POV; preserves id + indexes + collation. After this run, table wp_bizcity_cg_flows no longer exists anywhere in the install.
Fallback when interim wp_bizcity_cg_flows absent. INSERT...SELECT preserving id. Legacy table belongs to bizgpt-custom-flows plugin so we copy (do not rename).
